source_pr.title: executor: add tests for property pushed down for partition table
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/26166

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Add tests that we would not choose tiflash if we want to push down the prop.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: No explicit flaky failure signature is described in the PR or reflected in the patch; the changes add/extend tests around property pushdown for partition tables (including TiFlash) and minor supporting code tweaks.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (tests/feature coverage).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR mainly expands unit tests for partition table property pushdown (including TiFlash plan checks) and makes small supporting changes. The patch does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix for an intermittent failure, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: Add/extend partition table property pushdown tests (including TiFlash) and adjust supporting code; no targeted flaky stabilization evidence in patch.

evidence: evidence: queryPartitionWithTiFlash := fmt.Sprintf("select /*+ read_from_storage(tiflash[trange_intpk]) */ * from trange_intpk where a > %v order by a limit %v", x, y) (executor/partition_table_test.go)
